channel.title: career on Miranda Zhang's Website
channel.description: Recent content in career on Miranda Zhang's Website

item.title: Why I'm working at Anthropic
item.description:
There are four reasons I’m working at Anthropic: the potential for in/direct impact, upskilling, and career capital. I’ve put my weights out of 100% (note: these are ass numbers) in parentheses.

While I list key assumptions for each reason, I am assuming that you have background context on my cause priorities.

Upskilling (50%)

Testing my fit for operations and gaining skills

Assumptions

  1. [Some aspect of] Operations could be my comparative advantage, since I am a good fit for community-building and operations has overlapping skills.
  2. I haven’t been exposed to much operations work.
  3. Operations (in the EA generalist sense of, basically anything non-technical) is currently a bottleneck in the EA community, particularly for longtermist organisations.

The first three suggest that I should test my fit for operations, which is exactly what I’m doing! Some background context is that I fell in love with EA community-building in 2021 while organizing UChicago EA, but I don’t want to commit too quickly without seeing if I might be a better fit1 for something else.2

The fourth increases Anthropic’s appeal, since it’s a scrappy and expanding start-up. Anthropic seems particularly good for me because I suspect I am not that compatible with start-up culture… But, fortunately, I am joining at an unusual time when they are (a) still scrappy and looking for generalists that have to do everything, yet (b) have a decent operations team (of ~three) that are relatively more experienced. This means I can be exposed to loads of operations work but still have a supportive team that I can learn from (as opposed to supportive colleagues without operations experience).

Presumably, if I end up being an operations wonder, I can use this knowledge and experience to be an impact multiplier for organizations! (And if I don’t, oh well, I get to go back to my first love: multiplying my own impact by recruiting people for a community, not an organization.)

Strongest counterpoint(s)

  • I should be testing fit for non-Operations work instead.3
  • I should have just exploited my community-building skills.

Career Capital (20%)

Assumptions

  1. Working at Anthropic will make it easier for me to work at other, bigger AI labs (e.g., DeepMind). This is good for the reasons listed in indirect impact.
  2. Of my options, Anthropic carried relatively more status.

As an early-career person (literally, this is my first full-time job), I think it makes sense for me to place the most weight on upskilling and career capital, which I view as investments in my lifetime impact.

Strongest counterpoint(s)

  • I may not want to keep working at AI labs (e.g., if I decide to do general EA community-building instead)

Direct Impact (10%)

Increasing the probability that Anthropic is the first to build AGI

Assumptions

  1. Of the current ~5 actors most likely to build artificial general intelligence (AGI),4 Anthropic seems the most aligned with effective altruism (EA) principles and community. This implies they prioritize AI safety over capabilities.
  2. It seems possible—if not probable—that Anthropic will be the first to build AGI.
  3. Improving Anthropic’s operations will increase the probability that Anthropic will be the first to build AGI.

If these assumptions hold, then it seems good for me to help increase the possibility that the most aligned actor is the first to build AGI (e.g., by recruiting top engineers and researchers; reducing non-technical burdens on technical staff).

Strongest counterpoint(s)

  • Anthropic may not have a well-developed strategy for building (+ deploying) safe AGI, and so may be advancing capabilities work more than safety work.
  • Anthropic is unlikely to ‘win’ the AGI race.

Indirect Impact (20%)

Maintain Anthropic’s alignment with the EA and suffering-focused ethics (SFE) communities

Assumptions

  1. Anthropic’s team has relatively fewer EAs, or relatively weaker self-identification with the EA community, compared to organizations such as Redwood Research or MIRI.
  2. Organizational alignment can change depending on its team.
  3. Anthropic’s leadership has historically been open to considering s-risks, which is a potential top priority for the suffering-focused community.

It’s unclear how stable Anthropic’s alignment with EA is. While they are aligned, they don’t explicitly mention EA or related terms such as ’existential risk,’ (unlike Rethink Priorities, Legal Priorities Project, or Open Philanthropy).

I’ve also heard that Anthropic has been open to factoring s-risks into their thinking, which—as a very suffering-focused person—makes me more excited to work there.

Thus, being another EA- and SFE-aligned presence at Anthropic could help sustain its alignment with the EA and SFE communities.

Strongest counterpoint(s)

  • The counterfactual impact might be larger at a less-aligned organization (e.g., DeepMind, OpenAI).5
  • The absolute impact might be larger at a more-aligned organization.

  1. I’m using “best fit” to include all the factors of personal impact, ranging from personal fit to impact. ↩︎

  2. Classic explore vs exploit, you know. ↩︎

  3. I am sort of doing this for AI safety technical research, AI strategy, and s-risk/AI safety field-building. ↩︎

  4. In no particular order, I think this set contains DeepMind, OpenAI, Meta, Anthropic, and the Chinese government. ↩︎

  5. However, it also seems less tractable to influence a less-aligned organization. ↩︎

item.title: Ended the ~7-month job search!
item.description:
It’s been a wild, exhausting, and shocking ride. If I were more bullshitty, I might claim my job search as evidence of how things are heavy-tailed, since this ending is a result of ~3 weeks (aka ~11% of the total search).

Much love to the people in my life who spent hours with me trying to figure out what the highest-EV thing was to do, and to the people who validated my feelings that the highest-EV thing seemed stressful and personally risky.

But I guess we’re in the world where I’m doing the high-EV thing!!! I’m SO glad to put a pause to the job search, and I’m sure y’all will appreciate less career angst on your feeds. : )
